frameworks.name: summary statistical representation
frameworks.one_line: vision computes ensemble statistics (mean, variance) over object groups

frameworks.name: signal-detection theory
frameworks.one_line: precision-vs-capacity trade-off framing of ensemble readout

core_question: How does the visual system extract summary statistics from groups of similar objects, and what do those ensemble representations imply for the granularity of visual consciousness?
key_assumptions: summary statistics are computed across multiple levels of perceptual analysis
ensemble coding underpins gist and scene-level inference
conscious visual representation is bounded by ensemble granularity
manipulations: feature dimension over which the ensemble is taken (orientation, size, emotional expression, heading)
key_findings: ensemble statistics for multiple feature dimensions can be extracted in a brief glance
ensemble computation shows a characteristic precision-vs-capacity trade-off
ensembles support gist perception, scene categorization, and crowd-level inference
interpretations: ensemble perception sets foundational limits on cognition, memory, and visual awareness
the granularity of conscious visual representation reflects ensemble-coding limits
